content.full_text:
Memo re telephone advice: staff: 
Margaret Ellison 
To: 
Craig Primo, Campaign Manager, McClintock Committee 
Tel. No.: (805) 495-0405 
Date: 
8/22/89 
I called Mr. Primo in response to his letter of August 9, 
1989, requesting written confirmation of the effective date of the 
Taxpayers decision. 
I told him that the Commission would be 
considering an opinion request and possibly rendering an opinion 
at the 9/6/89 meeting. 
I told him that there was also a 
possibility of clarification from the Court of Appeal in response 
to our request for rehearing to clarify that issue. 
I advised him 
that we would not be responding in writing to requests for an 
effective date, because of the anticipated Commission opinion 
which would serve as written advice for everyone. 
I stated that 
based upon information received from the clerk's office and our 
subsequent research, we believed the earliest enforcement date to 
be 10/3/89. 


Post Office Oox 6.3.36 
Thousand Oaks, California 91.362 
(805) 495-0405 
A1iStmblu 
<nalifnmia 1Jjtgislaturt 
FPPC 
atten: 
John Larson 
428 J street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Mr. Larson: 
THOMAS McCLINTOCK 
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-SIXTH DISTRICT 
Assembly Republican Whip 
Vice Chairman 
Assembly Judiciary Committee 
August 9, 1989 
Committees: 
Ways and Means 
Judiciary 
Revenue and Taxation 
Educarion 
Policy Research 
Member, Flepublican 
Policy COmmiftee 
Member, Flepublican 
T osk Force on 
Tort Fleform 
-
f8 
I am writing to you today requesting written confirmation of the 
FPPC's position on a recent court ruling. 
The decision in 
question is the one handed down last week in the 2nd district 
count of appeals regarding off-year contributions. 
My question 
to you is this, what is the effective date of this decision. 
Thank you for your time. 
for bV McClintock Committee 
Walter Knauss, Treasurer. I.D. #820776 
Not Printed or Moiled at 
Expense

I told him that the Commission would be considering an opinion request and possibly rendering an opinion at the 9/6/89 meeting. I told him that there was also a possibility of clarification from the Court of Appeal in response to our request for rehearing to clarify that issue. I advised him that we would not be responding in writing to requests for an effective date, because of the anticipated Commission opinion which would serve as written advice for everyone. I stated that based upon information received from the clerk's office and our subsequent research, we believed the earliest enforcement date to be 10/3/89.
